adrs.title: Stack Choice
adrs.security_impact_summary: Stack must support server-side auth/role/scope checks, private storage, audit writer and protected APIs. Browser-submitted role, school_id, status and approval values remain forbidden.
adrs.rollback_impact_summary: Stack choice is high-cost to reverse after real module implementation. Before business-code build, it remains reversible.

adrs.title: Authentication Model
adrs.security_impact_summary: Critical. Disabled users must be blocked, sessions revocable, invite tokens hashed/expiring, and staff role/scope resolved server-side.
adrs.rollback_impact_summary: Auth changes are high-risk. Rollback should disable new auth pathway and preserve existing sessions only where safe.

adrs.title: RBAC, RLS and Scope Model
adrs.security_impact_summary: Critical. Browser role/scope/school_id is never trusted. Server must recheck every route and API.
adrs.rollback_impact_summary: If a policy bug is detected, disable affected feature flags and restrict access to super admins/security reviewers until fixed.

adrs.title: Database and Migration Strategy
adrs.security_impact_summary: High. Migration changes touching roles, payments, materials, results, certificates or audit require review.
adrs.rollback_impact_summary: Prefer forward-fix, old-column retention and feature flag rollback. Destructive rollback is discouraged.

adrs.title: File Storage and Signed URL Strategy
adrs.security_impact_summary: Critical for exam materials and exports. Signed URLs must be short-lived, not logged, and school/staff scoped.
adrs.rollback_impact_summary: Disable download flags, revoke/expire files, invalidate signed URLs and preserve metadata/audit.

adrs.title: Worker Queue Strategy
adrs.security_impact_summary: High. Workers run with system identity but must obey source-module policies and avoid leaking secrets/logging sensitive payloads.
adrs.rollback_impact_summary: Pause queues, disable feature flags, drain/retry/dead-letter safely and preserve job audit.

adrs.title: Audit Event Model
adrs.security_impact_summary: Critical. Raw audit access must be restricted; audit records must not leak sensitive payloads to normal users.
adrs.rollback_impact_summary: Audit events are not rolled back. Corrections are appended as new events.

adrs.title: Feature Flag Strategy
adrs.security_impact_summary: High. Public/exam/payment/result/export flags require approval and audit.
adrs.rollback_impact_summary: First rollback option is usually disabling the relevant feature flag.

adrs.title: Reports and Export Security
adrs.security_impact_summary: Critical. Exports must never include answer keys, raw OMR, provider payloads or private file URLs by default.
adrs.rollback_impact_summary: Expire generated files, disable sensitive exports flag and preserve export metadata/audit.

adrs.title: Public Verification Strategy
adrs.security_impact_summary: High. Prevent enumeration and detectability leaks through response shape.
adrs.rollback_impact_summary: Disable public verification flag and keep school/private certificate access intact.

adrs.title: Result and Certificate Versioning
adrs.security_impact_summary: Critical. Result publication/correction and certificate revocation/reissue require approval and audit.
adrs.rollback_impact_summary: Rollback is a new version, withhold, revoke or reissue; not deletion.

adrs.title: Deployment and Rollback Strategy
adrs.security_impact_summary: High. Production deployment must not bypass security/privacy checks or enable high-risk flags without approval.
adrs.rollback_impact_summary: Rollback uses feature flags first, then code rollback, then migration forward-fix/rollback depending on risk.
